data.title: BELIEF's market capitalization surpasses $20 million, with a daily increase of over 30%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of Sol's ecosystem token BELIEF has surged past $20 million, setting a new all-time high, currently trading at $17.8 million, a daily increase of over 30%. The token's narrative is based on the Proof of Belief (POB) model proposed by Printr V2, a project incubated by Bybit Venture Studio.

data.title: Spot gold has climbed above $4,750 per ounce, up 0.67% on the day.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold rose above $4,750 per ounce, up 0.67% on the day. Spot silver surged $1 to $77.69 per ounce, a gain of 1.32%. New York gold futures extended their gains to 1.00%, currently trading at $4,766.90 per ounce.

data.title: Analysts: AI funding reached $242 billion in Q1, but the funds were highly concentrated in a few "mega-funding rounds".
data.description: Crypto analyst AI disclosed data on the X platform, revealing that in the first quarter of 2026, companies in the field of artificial intelligence raised approximately $242 billion, accounting for about 80% of global venture capital investment. However, the funds were highly concentrated in a few "mega-rounds," such as OpenAI's $122 billion funding round, Anthropic's $30 billion, xAI's $20 billion, and Waymo's $16 billion. Meanwhile, enterprise-level AI spending continued to rise. Gartner predicts that global AI spending will reach $2.52 trillion in 2026, a year-on-year increase of 44%, with funding rapidly shifting from application layers to infrastructure and computing power construction.

data.title: FLORK's market capitalization surpasses $9 million, surging more than 21 times in a single day.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of the Ethereum blockchain's Meme coin, FLORK, has surged past $9 million, currently trading at $6 million, representing a more than 21-fold increase within a day. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong AI Concept Stocks Decline in Early Trading
data.description: Hong Kong AI concept stocks experienced a downturn at the start of trading. According to Jin10, Paradigm Intelligence (06682.HK) saw a significant drop of 10.38%, Kingsoft Cloud (03896.HK) fell by 4.67%, and Qunhe Technology (00068.HK) decreased by 2.54%.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Commerzbank Predicts Gold to Boost Silver Prices
data.description: Commerzbank has expressed its view that silver's fair value is closer to $40 rather than the current $80. According to Jin10, the bank also forecasts that gold prices will return to $5,000 as the U.S. Federal Reserve is expected to cut interest rates in the latter half of the year. This anticipated rise in gold prices is projected to push silver prices up to $90.

data.title: US lawmaker: I'd rather Trump play golf than go to the Situation Room
data.description: New York Democratic Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez stated that she would rather see Trump "on the golf course" than in the Oval Office. This statement came after Trump issued several strong threats against Iran. Cortez told reporters, "You know, obviously this guy is terrible at golf, but in a sense, we've seen some of the most important military decision-makers in the country trying to keep him away from major decisions. So, in a way, you'd even prefer this guy on the golf course to in the Oval Office. But this also raises some questions about the 25th Amendment, which suggests that if Trump is deemed untrustworthy in the Situation Room and in handling contingencies, then he's unfit to be president." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Michael Saylor: Strategy achieved a profit of 47,079 BTC in the first three weeks of April, a return of 6.2%.
data.description: Michael Saylor posted on the X platform that Strategy achieved a gain of 47,079 BTC in the first three weeks of April, a return of 6.2%, equivalent to approximately $3.6 billion at current prices. Saylor stated that Bitcoin gain is the closest metric to net income in the "Bitcoin standard" framework.

data.title: Eightco disclosed that its WLD holdings have increased to over 283 million, while its ETH holdings remain at 11,068.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Eightco Holdings (ORBS) released an update on its holdings, disclosing that its total asset value has increased to $336 million, including: 283,452,700 Worldcoin (WLD), 11,068 ETH, a $90 million investment in OpenAI, a $25 million investment in Beast Industries, and $118 million worth of cash and stablecoins. Eightco Holdings stated that its WLD token holdings now represent 23% of the company's total assets, its investment in OpenAI has decreased to 27%, and its investment in Beast Industries represents 7%. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. US SEC Chairman: Moving forward with the establishment of a digital asset regulatory framework and proposing the "A-C-T" strategy; 2. SpaceX announces it has the right to acquire AI programming startup Cursor for $60 billion; 3. Warsh hearing: Denies promising interest rate cuts to Trump, criticizes the Fed's misguided approach, and is called a "puppet"; 4. Digital bank Revolut's IPO valuation could reach as high as $200 billion; 5. KelpDAO hackers have exchanged most of their ETH for BTC through THORChain cross-chain technology; 6. The CLARITY bill may be rescheduled for a May hearing and a July vote, with a 50% chance of passing this year; 7. SpaceX drops a "IPO bombshell": Space AI technology is unproven and may not be commercializable; 8. Bloomberg: Li Lin transfers a 20-person trading team to Hong Kong-listed Bitfire; 9. Economic Daily: Resolutely curb illegal and irregular activities such as "over-the-counter" speculation; 10. Michael Saylor: Strategy achieved a profit of 47,079 BTC in the first three weeks of April, with a return of 6.2%.

data.title: Analysis: Gold is trading in a short-term range, lacking a strong upward trend.
data.description: CITIC Futures analysis points out that gold prices were weighed down by two factors: firstly, the impending expiration of the temporary ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, coupled with uncertain prospects for US-Iran negotiations, which weakened buying sentiment; and secondly, the tightening of market expectations for interest rate cuts due to Warsh's emphasis on maintaining the Fed's independence and low inflation during his hearing, and the better-than-expected rebound in March retail sales data. Firstly, the website of the US Senate Banking Committee shows that on April 21, Warsh, the nominee for Fed Chair, stated during his hearing that if appointed and head of the Fed, he would make independent monetary policy decisions, unaffected by any advice or pressure from Trump, and emphasized that low inflation is the Fed's shield. Secondly, US retail sales rose 1.7% month-on-month in March, exceeding the expected 1.4% and the previous value of 0.7%; core retail sales rose 1.9% month-on-month, also exceeding the expected 1.4% and the previous value of 0.7%. Supported by strong retail data, market expectations for Fed rate cuts further narrowed. Third, the temporary ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran is set to expire on April 22nd (Eastern Time). Trump has publicly stated that if the two sides cannot reach an agreement before the ceasefire expires, extending the ceasefire is "extremely unlikely." Iran has also stated its refusal to participate in a second round of negotiations with the US. The uncertain prospects for the negotiations have led to cautious buying sentiment in the gold market, with funds waiting for the situation to become clearer. (Jinshi)

data.title: Russia's State Duma passed the digital currency bill in its first reading, giving the central bank control over market access and transaction regulation.
data.description: The Russian State Duma (lower house of parliament) has passed the "Cryptocurrency and Digital Rights Bill" on its first reading, marking a key step towards the legalization of crypto assets in the country. According to the bill, the Bank of Russia will become the core regulatory body for the crypto market, responsible for issuing licenses, approving or prohibiting related transactions, and defining the legality of transactions. The bill proposes to recognize cryptocurrencies as "property," but explicitly prohibits their use as a means of payment domestically; the ruble will remain the sole legal tender. However, given Western sanctions, crypto assets can be used for cross-border trade settlements, including service payments and intellectual property transfers. Furthermore, the bill allows Russian residents to legally invest in crypto assets through licensed institutions, but will implement an investor tiered system, setting tests and annual investment limits for ordinary investors (a suggested cap of 300,000 rubles). Initially, only high-market-cap mainstream assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum will be allowed to trade, with the central bank creating a whitelist. The bill is expected to be formally passed and take effect no later than July 2026. However, some lawmakers and banking professionals have criticized the regulations as overly strict, potentially affecting market activity and even causing funds to remain in the gray market. Simultaneously, supporting legislation is proposed to introduce criminal penalties, with a maximum sentence of seven years imprisonment for illegal cryptocurrency transactions. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: Economic Daily: Resolutely curb illegal and irregular activities such as off-exchange trading of word components.
data.description: An article in the Economic Daily, titled "Making Good Use of my country's Word Element Advantages," points out the need to be aware of potential risks associated with word elements, including potential leaks leading to identity theft, forged permissions to steal sensitive data, and exploitative practices such as agents profiting from user commissions. Some criminals have begun targeting word elements, creating consumer traps disguised as "low-priced word element packages" or "word element agents." The article emphasizes the need to continuously improve policies, regulations, and standards, and to regulate word element trading by cracking down on price monopolies, false advertising, and illegal financial activities. It also stresses the importance of resolutely curbing illegal activities such as hoarding for appreciation and off-exchange speculation, guiding word elements back to their original purpose of providing technical services, value settlement, and the transfer of rights.

data.title: Bloomberg: Li Lin moves 20-person trading team to Hong Kong-listed company Bitfire
data.description: Li Lin is transferring his entire trading team of approximately 20 people from his family office, Avenir Group, to Bitfire Group, a Hong Kong-listed wealth management firm. Li Lin reportedly holds a 30% stake in Bitfire, and this move is intended to help the company attract more private banking clients. Bitfire CEO Livio Weng stated that Bitfire will acquire the team and its trading system from Avenir Group. Avenir confirmed the deal. With the addition of the new team, Bitfire has received initial investment intentions of approximately $500 million from family offices and publicly traded companies. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Documentary Claims to Uncover Satoshi Nakamoto's Identity
data.description: The creators of the documentary 'Finding Satoshi' assert that the film has conclusively identified the person behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. According to NS3.AI, the filmmakers have chosen not to disclose this information beyond the scope of the project. The documentary also explores Bitcoin's beginnings as a tool for privacy and notes that Satoshi is thought to possess approximately 1.1 million Bitcoin, which have remained untouched.

data.title: Jefferies: KelpDAO security incident may slow down Wall Street's blockchain deployment.
data.description: Wall Street investment bank Jefferies analysis points out that the Kelp DAO attack on April 18, which resulted in approximately $293 million in damage, exposed critical infrastructure risks and may prompt traditional financial institutions to reassess the pace of blockchain and tokenization development. Jefferies believes that the attackers triggered a market sell-off and liquidity shortage by minting uncollateralized tokens and engaging in cross-platform lending. The incident is believed to be potentially linked to Lazarus Group and also highlights the single point of failure in the verification mechanisms of cross-chain bridges. As institutions accelerate the tokenization of assets (such as funds, bonds, and deposits), related risks may cause some banks and asset management institutions to postpone deployment and prioritize system security. Especially in scenarios relying on cross-chain infrastructure, security vulnerabilities could lead to market fragmentation, weakening the actual utility of tokenized assets. Despite the short-term setback in confidence, Jefferies emphasizes that the long-term trend remains unchanged. With regulatory progress and continuous infrastructure improvement, stablecoins and other application scenarios still have growth potential, but the industry as a whole is still in its early stages of development and needs time to improve system robustness. (CoinDesk)

data.title: DDC Enterprise Reports Record Revenue and Launches AI-Driven Platform
data.description: DDC Enterprise, a publicly traded company on the U.S. stock market, has released its fiscal year 2025 earnings report, revealing a record revenue of $39.2 million, marking a 4.6% increase from the previous year. According to Odaily, the company holds approximately 2,383 bitcoins as of April 21, 2026, placing it among the top 30 global publicly listed companies in terms of bitcoin holdings. DDC Enterprise also announced the launch of its AI-driven platform, 'DDC Treasury Intelligence Platform,' designed to optimize bitcoin fund management and capital allocation.

data.title: Blockchain.com launches self-custodied perpetual contract trading, powered by Hyperliquid.
data.description: Blockchain.com announced the launch of perpetual contract trading functionality, powered by Hyperliquid, in its non-custodial DeFi wallet. This allows users to conduct leveraged trading directly within their own wallets without transferring assets to third-party exchanges, achieving full self-custodial control. (PRNewswire)

data.title: A whale sold off 2.9 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for ETH and BNB worth $1.17 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, one whale sold off 2.9 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for $1.17 million worth of ETH and BNB, making a profit of $1.16 million. Another whale sold off 3.39 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for $935,000 worth of ETH, making a profit of $865,000.

data.title: Trump plans to extend measures to ease restrictions on U.S. oil shipments.
data.description: Trump's suspension of the controversial Jones Act, a maritime law that facilitated the transport of oil within the United States during the Iran war, has now been extended—and he wants to maintain it. The 1920 Jones Act, a law that increased shipping costs between U.S. ports, required cargo to be transported by vessels flying the U.S. flag. In response to rising fuel prices due to the Iran war, Trump issued a 60-day waiver of the law on March 18 to streamline oil shipments. According to data provided by the White House, since then, 40 tankers have been able to transport oil between U.S. ports from California to Texas, Florida, and Alaska, increasing the actual available fleet size by 70% and helping to reduce costs. (Jinshi)
